evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
evidence.domain_risk: ensuring our data is clean, accessible, and ready for analysis
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt. Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: ensure our data infrastructure effectively supports self-serve analytics, insightful reporting, and business intelligence across the organisation
evidence.autonomy_level: collaborating closely with Product Analysts, Data Scientists, and Business Stakeholders to meet their analytical and reporting needs
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The quote covers both designing/building new data models and maintaining/optimising them for performance and scale, indicating mixed greenfield and fix/scale work.
evidence.ai_role: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Lists software engineering practices without mentioning AI tools or infrastructure requirements.
evidence.testing_framing: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Candidate owns testing as explicit software engineering practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensuring our data is clean, accessible, and ready for analysis
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Protective framing around data quality indicates operational reliability concern.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize testing, version control, continuous integration, data validation, and monitoring—core rigour practices—with no competing velocity signals in the job description.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Marketing analytics affects business decisions but lacks regulatory/compliance language and explicit financial reporting requirements.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift, Dagster/Airflow) in responsibilities indicate existing data stack requiring maintenance and optimization.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: This specificity about dbt models, SQL, software engineering practices, and designated domain ownership reflects hands-on technical knowledge only someone doing this work would articulate.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary mission is building and maintaining data infrastructure for internal analytical consumption, not serving commercial, product, or finance specifically.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role is framed around meeting defined needs of others rather than setting direction or defining analytics strategy.
